conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_005. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
My order ORD-6027 hasn't moved in over a week. Tracking hasn't updated since it shipped. Where is it?

I’ve checked your order, and the carrier has flagged this shipment as lost in transit, which is why the tracking hasn’t updated since it left the warehouse.  

I can either:  
1) Send a free replacement of your “Premium Cotton Shirt” right away, or  
2) Issue a full refund to your original payment method.  

Which option would you prefer?
I’d prefer a full refund back to my original payment method, please.

I’ve processed a full refund of $67 (including your original shipping) back to your credit card for order ORD-6027, since the package was lost in transit.  

You’ll see the refund on your statement within 3–10 business days, depending on your bank. If you’d still like the shirt, I can help you place a fresh order as well.

amount.full_refund: Full refund for defective, wrong item, or damaged in transit.
amount.promo_redistribution: If a promo/coupon was used: discount allocated proportionally by item price. Refund = item_price - (discount * item_price / subtotal).
amount.outside_window: Outside return window but within store-credit grace: store credit only.
amount.shipping_refund: When refunding shipping (defective/wrong/damaged returns, 6+-days-late compensation), refund the actual shipping cost the customer was charged on the order — read order.shipping_cost and refund that amount. Standard policy: $0 on free-shipping orders (subtotal >= $100), $8 otherwise. NOT for buyer's remorse.

method.original_payment: Default refund method when item was paid for and is being returned for fault.
method.store_credit: Required for: gift returns (at current product price), outside-window grace returns, and exchange-cheaper differences.
method.store_credit_only_constraint: When a return is issued under the store-credit-only rule (gift return OR outside-window grace), the refund method cannot be changed back to original_payment afterward. process_refund will reject any attempt to flip the method on those returns.

rules.price_match: If product price drops within 7 days of delivery, refund the difference (no return required).
rules.goodwill_credit: Goodwill credits (e.g., fragile-item damage bonus) are issued as a refund with the credit amount.

conversation.content:
Thanks for sorting that out and issuing the refund so clearly. That’s all I needed, I’ll skip reordering for now.  

[TASK_DONE]
